---
title: "Elaboration to the IR"
subtitle: "T1 · Phase T1.3 · `va-frontend::elaborate`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: code complete, real corpus coverage.** 120 tests, all green. Against the tracked
118-real-model + auxiliary-fragment corpus under `external/` (150 files total once every
sibling `` `include ``-only header/body fragment is counted), **114/150 pass the full
frontend** (`cargo run -p va-cli -- check external/`, confirmed just now, not assumed from a
stale count).
:::

## Goal

Turn a surface `ModuleAst` into the frozen arena IR (Interface α, `va-ir`): names resolved to
indices, constants folded, control flow lowered — the one and only place this crate touches
`va-ir` directly.

## Where it fits

```
 ModuleAst ──► [elaborate] ──va_ir::Module (Interface α)──► va-codegen
```

Elaboration is also where **module instantiation** happens for real: `elaborate_with_library`
takes the whole file's sibling modules as a lookup table and recursively inlines an
`Item::Instance`'s referenced submodule's own arenas into the instantiating module, so by the
time a `Module` reaches `va-codegen` it is always flat — `va-ir` itself never had to grow a
"module references another module" concept.

## The idea

Five ordered passes, each depending only on what came before it (`elaborate.rs`'s own doc
comment):

1. **Parameters** — const-evaluate every default and range bound to `f64` first, so a vector
   net's `[msb:lsb]` range can reference one.
2. **Genvars** — register every declared `genvar` name before variables are collected, so a
   genvar's own loop header is never mistaken for an ordinary analog variable.
3. **Nodes** — intern every net that carries a discipline declaration (expanding a vector net's
   range into one node per index); resolve the port list.
4. **Variables** — register explicit `real`/`integer` declarations, then every remaining
   assignment target, as local variables (skipping genvars).
5. **Lowering** — translate the analog block's expressions/statements, creating branches on
   demand as accesses resolve. A `for` whose header assigns a genvar is fully unrolled here into
   a flat `Stmt::Block` (§ `generate` loops); every other `for` lowers to a runtime `Stmt::For`.

**Two const-eval paths, not one.** Parameter defaults/ranges/genvar bounds go through a separate,
non-mutating `const_eval` — distinct from the main analog-block lowering (`lower_expr`) — since
they must be resolvable *before* the circuit has a solution at all. A handful of builtins
(`$simparam`, mainly) needed the identical "fold to the default argument, or error if none"
treatment added to both paths, not just one.

**A DC-only simulator can still soundly fold time-domain builtins**, one at a time, each
verified against the specific case that needed it: `transition`/`slew`/`absdelay` settle to
their undelayed input at a fixed operating point (no delay history exists at DC); the eight
Laplace/Z-domain filter builtins (`laplace_nd`/`np`/`zd`/`zp`, `zi_*`) settle to their own DC
(`s=0`) or steady-state (`z=1`) gain, derived from the LRM's own normative formulas, not memory
— cross-checked against the LRM's own worked example (`laplace_zp('{-1,0}, '{-1,-1,-1,1})` → gain
`1`); the random-distribution family (`$rdist_uniform`, …) folds to its own distribution's mean,
since v0 has no RNG to actually draw a sample. None of these are approximations of convenience —
each is the textbook DC/steady-state limit of the real time-domain behavior.

## The code

```rust
pub fn elaborate_with_library_and_disciplines(
    ast: &ModuleAst,
    library: &[ModuleAst],
    disciplines: &[DisciplineDecl],
    natures: &[NatureDecl],
) -> Result<va_ir::Module, FrontendError>
```

`compile_with_includes` (the crate's front door) calls this once per module in a file, always
passing the *whole* file's module list as `library` — so any `Item::Instance` anywhere in the
file resolves, regardless of which module ends up used as a device model. `compile`/`elaborate`
(no library/no disciplines) are thin wrappers with empty tables, kept only so a net with no
resolvable metadata still gets `abstol: None`, exactly prior behavior.

## It works

```console
$ cargo test -p va-frontend elaborate
running 120 tests
test elaborate::tests::zi_zp_real_roots_use_one_minus_root_not_one_minus_s_over_root ... ok
...
test result: ok. 120 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 96 filtered out; finished in 0.01s

$ cargo run -q -p va-cli -- check external/
114/150 files passed the frontend (lex → parse → elaborate)
```

Every one of the 36 remaining failures is categorized, not a mystery: ~20 are auxiliary
`` `include ``-only fragments never meant to compile standalone (`constants.vams`,
`disciplines.vams`, macro-definition headers); 8 more are top-level files whose entire module
body lives in a sibling `.include` file this corpus snapshot doesn't ship; the rest are the real,
tracked backlog below. `docs/roadmap.md`'s "Language coverage" section is the append-only,
dated changelog of every gap closed to get from the first corpus pass to 114/150 — each entry
names the specific file it unblocked and how, not a vague "improved coverage."

## Limitations

The real, currently-open backlog (`docs/roadmap.md`'s own prioritized list):

1. **Array-variable arguments to the Laplace/Z-domain filters.** Every filter builtin above only
   accepts a literal `{...}` array; `external/verilogaLib-master/ctle.va` instead assigns a
   declared array *variable* element-by-element before passing it. Supporting this needs a real
   capability this codebase doesn't have anywhere else: tracing a variable's value through its
   own straight-line assignment statements at elaboration time.
2. **Time-history-dependent event functions** (`last_crossing`, real `cross`/`timer`/`edge`
   semantics) — can't be soundly DC-folded the way `transition`/`slew` can, since their whole
   purpose is time history. `va-transient` now exists, but nothing in Interface β lets a
   `ModelInstance::load` call see its own history at all — a design question, not an engine gap.
3. **`reference_node`'s hardcoded-electrical implicit ground** — a bare single-terminal access's
   implicit second terminal is always tagged `Discipline::Electrical`, regardless of the
   access's own discipline (e.g. a bare `Temp(dt)`).

Permanently out of scope, not a backlog item (LRM Annex C.7 excludes these from Verilog-A
itself): gate/switch-level primitives, net strength/charge-storage keywords, and digital
procedural/timing constructs (`always`/`initial`/`fork`/`task`/`casex`/`casez`/…).

## What's next

`t2-codegen/01-ad-core.qmd` — differentiating the IR this phase produces.
